items.title: Extortion is latest Russian war tactic targeting Ukraine families
Lebanese soldier said killed in Israeli strike, Hezbollah fires 2 rockets at north amid truce
items.description: Fire on Galilee is latest ceasefire violation by Iran-backed terror group; Lebanese media reports dozens of strikes, home demolitions across south Lebanon; security cabinet meets at Kirya The post Lebanese soldier said killed in Israeli strike, Hezbollah fires 2 rockets at north amid truce appeared first on The Times o…

items.title: Greetings from Syria, where a postwar olive harvest offers a long-lost taste of home
items.description: In the warm sun, gathering handfuls of hard olives promised a taste of home that residents of a village in the Homs countryside had been missing for nearly 14 years of civil war.

items.title: ‘We saw terrible things’: Mali refugees tell of atrocities amid attacks
items.description: Thousands of Malians have been forced to flee their country as several groups, including the army, assault them.

items.title: Afghanistan: Shiite and other minorities living in fear
items.description: An attack on a Shiite place of worship in Herat killed least 11 people. While the Taliban have pledged protection, a sense of structural vulnerability is growing within the Shiite community.

items.title: ‘Total peace’ or ‘all-out war’? Colombian voters face stark choice as rebel attacks surge
items.description: As the country prepares to elect a new president, a fierce debate is raging on how to end the decades-long armed conflict for good The landmark 2016 peace deal between the Colombian government and the largest insurgent army in Latin America succeeded in some ways: the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc) agree…

items.title: Mali's Tuareg rebels vow regime 'will fall', urge Russian forces to withdraw
items.description: A spokesperson for Mali's Tuareg rebel group Azawad Liberation Front pledged on Wednesday that the country's ruling junta "will fall" and said the group wanted to see Russian forces withdraw "from all of Mali" after weekend attacks by Islamist insurgents and Tuareg separatists targeting major cities.

items.title: Malian jihadists and rebels ramp up pressure on junta: 'We are blocking Bamako'
items.description: Jihadists from the Al Qaeda-affiliated group JNIM announced a total siege of the Malian capital on Tuesday. At least five towns in the country's north have fallen to the attackers. 'The situation is under control,' President Assimi Goita said.

items.title: What next for Mali's military leaders after shock of rebel offensive?
items.description: The military came to power to bolster security but the weekend's attacks have brought that into question.

items.title: Von der Leyen, 'guerra in Iran costa all'Ue 500 milioni al giorno per energia'
items.description: 'In due mesi +27 miliardi per l'import, gli effetti potrebbero pesare per anni'

items.title: Their nation at war, Israelis still demand to know: How could Oct. 7 happen?
items.description: Amid wars with Iran and Hezbollah, Israelis still very much want an independent state commission of inquiry to learn the truth about the failure to prevent the Hamas attack that sparked the Gaza war. Israel’s top court has given the Netanyahu government more time.

items.title: Kremlin Calls for Stability in Mali as Rebels Seize Kidal and Target Capital
items.description: Moscow said it did not know the current status or whereabouts of Assimi Goïta, the leader of Mali’s military junta.

items.title: North Korea Is Honoring Some Of Its Soldiers Killed In The Ukraine War. But What About The Rest?
items.description: Why a sprawling war memorial that was opened on April 26 commemorates only a fraction of the estimated thousands of troops Pyongyang lost in Russia.

items.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Gaza ceasefire is ‘increasingly fragile’, UN warns
items.description: The Security Council turns again to the Middle East crisis in New York on Tuesday, with debate focusing on the fragile Gaza ceasefire, a worsening humanitarian crisis there and efforts to advance a US-backed peace plan.UN senior political affairs official Khaled Khiari warned of increasingly dire conditions in Gaza and…

items.title: Russia Uses Foliage as Cover to Probe Ukrainian Lines: New Twist in Battlefield Tactics
items.description: Ukrainian search‑and‑strike operations are containing Russian advances across many frontline areas, as troops attempt to use spring foliage to hide their movements.
